title: Chief Publishing Officer
summary: The Chief Publishing Officer turns Neuroartan’s writing, research, and institutional knowledge into clear, controlled public releases.
biography: This executive seat is open. Neuroartan has defined the role before appointing a public holder so the standard is clear from the beginning.
The role protects editorial quality, catalog structure, rights discipline, and the way knowledge moves from internal work into public form.
This profile remains role-first until a human executive is hired, verified, and approved for publication.
mandate: Make Neuroartan’s knowledge publishable: clear, accurate, owned, and released with the discipline required for public trust.
